articles.headline: Punjab Grapples with Devastating Floods: Calls for Enhanced Disaster Management and Climate Adaptation
articles.explainer:
• Heavy rainfall over several days has led to widespread flooding across Punjab, affecting multiple districts and causing significant loss of life and property.
• At least 30 fatalities have been reported, with thousands displaced and extensive damage to agricultural land and infrastructure.
• The state government has launched extensive relief and rescue operations, deploying NDRF and SDRF teams.
• Experts highlight the role of climate change, inadequate drainage systems, and encroachments on floodplains in exacerbating the disaster.
• Discussions are underway regarding long-term strategies for flood control, early warning systems, and climate-resilient infrastructure.
gsSubTopics: Disaster and disaster management
Environmental pollution and degradation
Conservation
Climate change
prelimsPoints: Location of affected areas in Punjab.
Role of NDRF and SDRF in disaster response.
Causes of flooding: heavy rainfall, drainage issues, climate change.
Government initiatives for flood control and relief.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze India's disaster management framework in the context of recent floods, suggesting improvements.
Discuss the interlinkages between climate change, urban planning, and increasing frequency of extreme weather events in India.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current infrastructure in mitigating flood risks and propose strategies for building climate-resilient infrastructure.
Examine the challenges in inter-state cooperation for river basin management and flood control.
articles.imageDescription: Aerial view of flooded agricultural fields and submerged villages in Punjab.
articles.trivia: The Bhakra Dam, one of the largest multi-purpose dams in India, is located on the Sutlej River in Punjab and Himachal Pradesh, playing a crucial role in flood moderation and irrigation.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Deliberates on Transparency in Judicial Appointments: A Step Towards Collegium Reforms?
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court heard arguments on petitions seeking greater transparency and a more merit-based system for judicial appointments.
• Discussions centered on the need for a balanced approach that maintains judicial independence while ensuring accountability and public trust.
• Petitioners argued for clearer criteria, public disclosure of selection reasons, and a more diverse representation in the judiciary.
• The government's stance emphasized the need for a robust mechanism, potentially revisiting aspects of the National Judicial Appointments Commission (NJAC) debate.
• A verdict is anticipated which could significantly influence the future of the Collegium system.
gsSubTopics: Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary
Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies
prelimsPoints: Know about the Collegium system and its evolution.
Understand the NJAC and its fate.
Constitutional provisions related to appointment of judges.
Difference between appointment of SC and HC judges.
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate the Collegium system for judicial appointments in India, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses.
Discuss the various proposals for reforming judicial appointments, considering both judicial independence and accountability.
Examine the role of the judiciary in upholding constitutional principles amidst debates on its own structure and functioning.
Analyze the implications of greater transparency in judicial appointments for public trust and the quality of justice.
articles.imageDescription: Supreme Court of India building in New Delhi.
articles.trivia: The 'Basic Structure Doctrine' of the Indian Constitution was propounded by the Supreme Court in the Kesavananda Bharati case in 1973.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Inflationary Concerns and Global Slowdown
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) concluded its latest Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) meeting, opting to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged.
• The decision comes against a backdrop of persistent core inflation, though food inflation shows signs of moderation.
• RBI Governor highlighted concerns over global economic uncertainties and their potential impact on India's growth trajectory.
• The central bank reaffirmed its commitment to withdrawing accommodation gradually to ensure inflation remains within the target band.
• Analysts predict a cautious stance will continue as the RBI navigates between supporting growth and taming inflation.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary policy
prelimsPoints: Functions of RBI and MPC.
Definition of Repo Rate, Reverse Repo Rate, CRR, SLR.
Tools of monetary policy.
Inflation targets set by RBI.
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control and economic growth in the current global economic scenario.
Discuss the effectiveness of monetary policy tools in addressing supply-side inflation in India.
Examine the coordination between fiscal and monetary policy in achieving macroeconomic stability.
Evaluate the impact of global economic slowdowns on India's monetary policy decisions.
articles.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India building in Mumbai.
articles.trivia: The Reserve Bank of India was established on April 1, 1935, in accordance with the provisions of the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

articles.headline: India-Africa Economic Forum Concludes, Pledges Enhanced Cooperation in Trade and Technology
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-Africa Economic Forum concluded with a joint declaration emphasizing deeper collaboration in critical sectors.
• Key areas of focus included digital transformation, renewable energy, healthcare, and agricultural development.
• India reaffirmed its commitment to capacity building and technology transfer to African nations.
• Discussions also covered improving trade logistics, reducing tariff barriers, and promoting private sector investments.
• The forum aims to strengthen India's strategic partnerships across the African continent.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: Key pillars of India-Africa cooperation.
Major Indian initiatives in Africa (e.g., Pan-Africa e-network project).
Importance of Africa for India's energy security and market access.
Recent India-Africa summits and declarations.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolving contours of India-Africa relations, highlighting the shift from traditional aid to partnership-based cooperation.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities for India in expanding its economic and strategic footprint in Africa.
Examine how India's 'development partnership' model differs from that of other major global powers in Africa.
Evaluate the potential of digital and green transitions to drive India-Africa collaboration.
articles.imageDescription: Delegates from India and African nations at a conference.
articles.trivia: India is one of the largest contributors of troops to UN peacekeeping missions in Africa.

articles.headline: ISRO's Shukrayaan-1 Mission Successfully Enters Venusian Orbit, Begins Scientific Observations
articles.explainer:
• India's maiden mission to Venus, Shukrayaan-1, has successfully performed the crucial Venus Orbit Insertion (VOI) maneuver.
• The spacecraft is now orbiting Venus and has commenced its primary scientific objectives, including studying the planet's atmosphere and surface.
• The mission aims to investigate Venus's atmospheric composition, surface features, and subsurface structure using advanced instruments.
• This achievement marks a significant milestone for ISRO, expanding its planetary exploration capabilities.
• Data from Shukrayaan-1 is expected to provide valuable insights into planetary evolution and habitability.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Space
prelimsPoints: Objectives of Shukrayaan-1 mission.
Key instruments on board Shukrayaan-1.
About Venus: 'Earth's twin', atmospheric composition.
Other countries' missions to Venus.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of India's Venus mission for its space program and global scientific community.
Examine how space exploration contributes to scientific knowledge and technological advancement, with specific reference to ISRO's achievements.
Analyze the challenges involved in interplanetary missions and how ISRO has overcome them.
Evaluate the geopolitical implications of India's growing capabilities in space technology.
articles.imageDescription: Artistic rendering of ISRO's Shukrayaan-1 spacecraft orbiting Venus.
articles.trivia: Venus rotates in the opposite direction to most other planets in our solar system, a phenomenon known as retrograde rotation.

articles.headline: National Green Hydrogen Mission Reports Significant Progress in Pilot Projects and Policy Framework
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy announced substantial progress in the implementation of the National Green Hydrogen Mission.
• Several pilot projects for green hydrogen production and utilization, including in steel and mobility sectors, have commenced operations.
• The government is finalizing incentives and regulatory frameworks to attract private investment and boost domestic manufacturing of electrolysers.
• The mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, contributing to energy security and decarbonization.
• Challenges related to cost-effectiveness, infrastructure development, and water availability for electrolysis remain key areas of focus.
gsSubTopics: Environmental pollution and degradation
Conservation
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: Objectives of the National Green Hydrogen Mission.
Difference between green, blue, and grey hydrogen.
Applications of green hydrogen (e.g., fertilizers, steel, transport).
Key technologies involved in green hydrogen production.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and climate change commitments.
Analyze the challenges in scaling up green hydrogen production and adoption in India, suggesting policy interventions.
Evaluate the potential of green hydrogen to transform key industrial sectors and its role in achieving net-zero targets.
Examine the geopolitical implications of India becoming a major player in the global green hydrogen market.
articles.imageDescription: An illustration depicting green hydrogen production facility with wind turbines and solar panels.
articles.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, constituting roughly 75% of all baryonic mass.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Standing Committee Recommends Comprehensive Review of MSP System for Crop Diversification
articles.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee submitted its report on agricultural reforms, advocating for a comprehensive review of the Minimum Support Price (MSP) mechanism.
• The report suggests linking MSP to crop diversification efforts and promoting cultivation of less water-intensive crops.
• It highlighted the need to address regional imbalances in MSP procurement and ensure benefits reach all farmers.
• Recommendations include strengthening agricultural market infrastructure and exploring alternative income support schemes.
• The committee emphasized a consultative approach involving farmers' organizations in policy formulation.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints
E-technology in the aid of farmers
Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices
keyTerms: Commission for Agricultural Costs and Prices (CACP)
prelimsPoints: How MSP is determined and by whom (CACP).
Number of crops under MSP.
Objectives of MSP.
Challenges associated with the current MSP system.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the role of MSP in ensuring farmers' income and food security in India, highlighting its unintended consequences.
Discuss the feasibility and potential benefits of linking MSP to crop diversification and sustainable agricultural practices.
Examine the reforms needed in India's agricultural marketing system to ensure better price realization for farmers and reduce post-harvest losses.
Evaluate alternative income support mechanisms for farmers in the context of MSP reforms.
articles.imageDescription: Farmers working in a field with various crops.
articles.trivia: The concept of MSP was introduced in India in the mid-1960s to incentivize farmers for adopting high-yielding varieties of crops during the Green Revolution.

articles.headline: Government Unveils New National Cyber Security Framework to Bolster Digital Defenses
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) launched a comprehensive National Cyber Security Framework.
• The framework aims to create a secure and resilient cyber ecosystem by establishing new guidelines for critical infrastructure protection, data governance, and incident response.
• It emphasizes public-private partnerships, skill development, and international cooperation to combat evolving cyber threats.
• The policy introduces stricter compliance requirements for organizations handling sensitive data and mandates regular security audits.
• This move is crucial for safeguarding India's digital economy and national security interests.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security
Money-laundering and its prevention
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism
prelimsPoints: Key objectives of the new framework.
Role of CERT-In (Indian Computer Emergency Response Team).
Concept of Critical Information Infrastructure (CII).
Major cyber threats faced by India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the increasing complexities of cyber threats and the need for a robust national cyber security framework in India.
Analyze the key provisions of the new framework and its potential impact on data governance and critical infrastructure protection.
Examine the role of public-private partnerships in building a resilient cyber ecosystem in India.
Evaluate the challenges in implementing a comprehensive cyber security strategy, including skill gaps and cross-border cooperation.
articles.imageDescription: A digital representation of a secure network with interconnected nodes.
articles.trivia: The first computer virus, 'Brain', was created in 1986 by two Pakistani brothers and targeted IBM PC compatible computers.

articles.headline: Review Meeting Assesses National Education Policy Implementation Progress and Challenges
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education held a high-level review meeting to assess the implementation status of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
• Discussions focused on the transition to multidisciplinary education, vocational training integration, and early childhood care and education (ECCE).
• Challenges identified include infrastructure gaps, faculty training, and ensuring equitable access to quality education, especially in rural areas.
• States presented their progress reports, highlighting innovative approaches and areas requiring further central support.
• The government reiterated its commitment to achieving the NEP's transformative goals by 2030.
gsSubTopics: Development processes and the development industry—the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
prelimsPoints: Key pillars and objectives of NEP 2020.
Recommendations for school and higher education.
Focus on ECCE and vocational education.
Role of technology in NEP implementation.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the transformative potential of NEP 2020 in addressing the challenges of India's education system.
Discuss the key challenges in the effective implementation of NEP 2020, particularly concerning infrastructure, faculty readiness, and equitable access.
Examine how the integration of vocational education and multidisciplinary learning can enhance employability and human resource development.
Evaluate the role of state governments and private institutions in achieving the goals of the National Education Policy.
articles.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom setting.
articles.trivia: The first National Policy on Education in India was promulgated in 1968, based on the recommendations of the Kothari Commission (1964-66).

articles.headline: Government Announces Accelerated Infrastructure Spending to Boost Economic Growth and Job Creation
articles.explainer:
• The Finance Ministry unveiled plans to significantly accelerate infrastructure spending in key sectors like roads, railways, and urban development.
• The move aims to stimulate economic activity, create employment opportunities, and enhance India's global competitiveness.
• Emphasis will be on projects with high multiplier effects and those that integrate green technologies and sustainable practices.
• Public-private partnerships (PPPs) are expected to play a crucial role, with new policy incentives for private sector participation.
• This initiative aligns with India's long-term vision of becoming a $5 trillion economy.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Investment models
prelimsPoints: Key sectors targeted for infrastructure spending.
Concept of PPPs and their importance.
Government initiatives like National Infrastructure Pipeline (NIP) and PM Gati Shakti.
Impact of infrastructure on GDP and employment.
mainsPoints: Discuss the critical role of infrastructure development in achieving India's economic growth targets and improving quality of life.
Analyze the challenges in financing and executing large-scale infrastructure projects in India, and suggest solutions.
Evaluate the effectiveness of public-private partnerships (PPPs) as a model for infrastructure development in the Indian context.
Examine how sustainable and green infrastructure can contribute to both economic development and environmental protection.
articles.imageDescription: Construction site of a modern highway with heavy machinery.
articles.trivia: India has the second-largest road network in the world, spanning over 6.3 million kilometers.

articles.headline: India Reiterates Call for Comprehensive UN Reforms Ahead of General Assembly Session
articles.explainer:
• India's External Affairs Minister reiterated the country's long-standing demand for comprehensive reforms of the United Nations, particularly the Security Council.
• The call comes ahead of the upcoming UN General Assembly session, where the issue of multilateralism and global governance is expected to be a key theme.
• India emphasized the need for a more representative and equitable Security Council that reflects contemporary geopolitical realities.
• The statement highlighted the growing consensus among developing nations for reforms that enhance the legitimacy and effectiveness of the UN.
• India's bid for a permanent seat in the UNSC remains a central aspect of its foreign policy objectives.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
prelimsPoints: Structure and functions of the UN Security Council.
Current permanent and non-permanent members of UNSC.
Arguments for and against UNSC expansion.
Role of G4 nations in UN reforms.
mainsPoints: Discuss the rationale behind India's persistent demand for reforms in the United Nations Security Council.
Analyze the challenges and prospects for comprehensive UN reforms in the current geopolitical landscape.
Examine how a reformed UNSC could better address contemporary global challenges such as climate change, pandemics, and terrorism.
Evaluate the implications of India gaining a permanent seat in the UNSC for its foreign policy and global influence.
articles.imageDescription: United Nations Headquarters building in New York.
articles.trivia: The United Nations was founded in 1945 after World War II to prevent future wars, replacing the ineffective League of Nations.

articles.headline: Urban Local Bodies Launch New Initiatives to Combat Air Pollution in Major Cities
articles.explainer:
• Several urban local bodies across India have rolled out enhanced action plans to improve air quality in their respective cities.
• Initiatives include deploying advanced air quality monitoring systems, promoting electric vehicles, and stricter enforcement of industrial emission norms.
• Focus is also on managing construction and demolition waste, controlling vehicular pollution, and increasing green cover.
• Public awareness campaigns are being intensified to encourage citizen participation in pollution reduction efforts.
• These measures are part of the broader National Clean Air Programme (NCAP) goals.
gsSubTopics: Environmental pollution and degradation
Conservation
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: Objectives of NCAP.
Components of Air Quality Index (AQI) and its categories.
Major sources of urban air pollution.
Government policies to control air pollution (e.g., BS norms, FAME India scheme).
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted challenges of urban air pollution in India and its impact on public health and the environment.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current government initiatives, such as NCAP, in achieving cleaner air targets.
Examine the role of urban local bodies and citizen participation in developing and implementing effective air pollution control strategies.
Suggest innovative technological and policy solutions to combat air pollution in Indian cities.
articles.imageDescription: A cityscape shrouded in smog with air quality monitoring equipment in the foreground.
articles.trivia: Delhi has consistently ranked among the most polluted cities globally, prompting various emergency measures like the 'odd-even' road rationing scheme.

articles.headline: NITI Aayog Convenes Stakeholders for Deliberations on India's AI Ethics Framework
articles.explainer:
• NITI Aayog hosted a crucial meeting with experts, industry leaders, and civil society organizations to discuss the draft AI Ethics Framework.
• The framework aims to ensure responsible and ethical development and deployment of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in India.
• Key principles under discussion include fairness, transparency, accountability, privacy protection, and prevention of bias in AI systems.
• Participants emphasized the need for a balanced approach that fosters innovation while safeguarding societal values and individual rights.
• The final framework is expected to guide policy-making and regulatory measures for AI governance.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights
prelimsPoints: Role of NITI Aayog in AI strategy.
Key ethical concerns surrounding AI (e.g., bias, privacy, job displacement).
Applications of AI in various sectors.
Global efforts in AI governance.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ethical dilemmas and societal implications posed by the rapid advancement of Artificial Intelligence.
Analyze the key principles that an effective AI Ethics Framework for India should encompass, balancing innovation and regulation.
Examine the challenges in implementing ethical AI guidelines across diverse applications and industries.
Evaluate the role of government, industry, and civil society in fostering responsible AI development and deployment.
articles.imageDescription: Stylized graphic representing AI algorithms and ethical considerations.
articles.trivia: The term 'Artificial Intelligence' was coined by John McCarthy in 1956 at the Dartmouth Conference, considered the birth of AI as a field.

articles.headline: Centre-State Fiscal Relations Under Scrutiny: Debate on Autonomy and Equity in Fiscal Transfers
articles.explainer:
• A renewed debate has emerged regarding the existing framework of fiscal transfers between the Centre and states, particularly concerning grants and centrally sponsored schemes.
• Several states have voiced concerns over reduced fiscal autonomy and conditionalities attached to central funds.
• The discussion highlights the recommendations of previous Finance Commissions and the need for a balanced approach to federal fiscal relations.
• Experts are deliberating on mechanisms to ensure greater equity in resource distribution while incentivizing fiscal discipline among states.
• The role of GST compensation and its future implications for state finances is also a key point of contention.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein
prelimsPoints: Constitutional provisions for Centre-State financial relations.
Role and functions of the Finance Commission.
Difference between grants-in-aid and shared taxes.
Impact of GST on state finances.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the evolution of fiscal federalism in India, highlighting the challenges in ensuring both fiscal autonomy for states and national uniformity.
Discuss the recommendations of recent Finance Commissions regarding fiscal transfers and their effectiveness in addressing inter-state disparities.
Examine the implications of the Goods and Services Tax (GST) regime on the fiscal powers and responsibilities of state governments.
Suggest reforms to the Centre-state fiscal relations framework to foster cooperative federalism and equitable development.
articles.imageDescription: Indian Parliament building, symbolizing federal governance.
articles.trivia: Article 280 of the Indian Constitution provides for the establishment of a Finance Commission.

articles.headline: Government Launches New Scheme to Enhance Healthcare Access in Remote Rural Areas
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare introduced a new comprehensive scheme aimed at bridging the healthcare gap in remote and underserved rural areas.
• The scheme focuses on strengthening primary healthcare centers, deploying mobile medical units, and leveraging telemedicine technologies.
• Special incentives are being offered to medical professionals to serve in rural postings, addressing the critical shortage of healthcare staff.
• Community health workers will receive enhanced training and resources to deliver preventive care and health education.
• The initiative is part of India's commitment to achieving universal health coverage and improving health outcomes for all citizens.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
prelimsPoints: Key components of the new scheme.
Challenges in rural healthcare delivery in India.
Government flagship health schemes (e.g., Ayushman Bharat, National Health Mission).
Role of technology in improving healthcare access.
mainsPoints: Discuss the persistent challenges in providing equitable and quality healthcare access to India's rural population.
Analyze the potential of telemedicine and mobile medical units in transforming rural healthcare delivery.
Examine the effectiveness of government schemes and incentives in attracting and retaining medical professionals in rural areas.
Suggest comprehensive strategies to achieve universal health coverage in India, considering both infrastructure and human resource development.
articles.imageDescription: A mobile medical unit serving patients in a remote Indian village.
articles.trivia: India is home to one of the world's largest public health programs, the National Health Mission (NHM), which encompasses the National Rural Health Mission (NRHM) and National Urban Health Mission (NUHM).

articles.headline: Border Roads Organisation Accelerates Infrastructure Development Along Northern Borders
articles.explainer:
• The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) announced accelerated progress on strategic infrastructure projects along India's northern borders.
• These projects include the construction of all-weather roads, bridges, and tunnels, crucial for military mobility and connectivity to remote areas.
• The development aims to enhance national security, facilitate trade, and improve the socio-economic conditions of border populations.
• Advanced construction techniques and indigenous materials are being utilized to overcome challenging terrain and harsh weather conditions.
• This renewed focus is part of a broader strategy to strengthen border management and integrate border regions into the national mainstream.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: Mandate and functions of BRO.
Importance of border infrastructure for national security.
Key passes and strategic locations in northern borders.
Challenges in border area development.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of robust border infrastructure for India's national security and economic development.
Analyze the challenges faced by organizations like BRO in constructing infrastructure in mountainous and remote border regions.
Examine how improved border connectivity can enhance military logistics, facilitate local trade, and integrate border populations.
Evaluate the geopolitical implications of India's accelerated border infrastructure development, particularly concerning neighboring countries.
articles.imageDescription: BRO workers constructing a road in a high-altitude mountainous region.
articles.trivia: The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) was formed in 1960 and constructs and maintains road networks in border areas and friendly neighboring countries.

articles.headline: Government Unveils New Incentives and Schemes for MSME Sector Growth and Competitiveness
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSME) announced a fresh package of incentives and schemes to bolster the growth and competitiveness of the sector.
• Key initiatives include easier access to credit, technology upgradation support, and enhanced market linkages for MSMEs.
• Special focus is on promoting digital adoption, skill development, and encouraging MSMEs to participate in global value chains.
• The government aims to increase the MSME sector's contribution to GDP and create millions of new jobs.
• Simplified regulatory compliance and ease of doing business measures are also part of the new package.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Industrial policy changes and their effects on industrial growth
keyTerms: Credit Guarantee Fund Trust for Micro and Small Enterprises (CGTMSE)
prelimsPoints: Definition of MSMEs based on investment and turnover.
Contribution of MSMEs to India's GDP and employment.
Government schemes for MSMEs (e.g., MUDRA, CGTMSE).
Challenges faced by the MSME sector.
mainsPoints: Discuss the critical role of the MSME sector in India's economic growth, employment generation, and inclusive development.
Analyze the key challenges faced by MSMEs in India, particularly concerning access to credit, technology, and markets.
Evaluate the effectiveness of recent government initiatives and policy reforms aimed at strengthening the MSME sector.
Suggest further measures to enhance the competitiveness of Indian MSMEs and integrate them into global supply chains.
articles.imageDescription: Workers in a small manufacturing unit.
articles.trivia: MSMEs contribute approximately 30% to India's GDP and over 45% to its manufacturing output and exports.

articles.headline: New National Project Launched for Integrated Wetland Conservation and Management
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change launched a new national project focused on integrated conservation and management of wetlands across India.
• The initiative aims to restore degraded wetlands, enhance their ecological services, and promote sustainable livelihood practices for local communities.
• It emphasizes stakeholder participation, scientific research, and capacity building for effective wetland management.
• Wetlands are crucial ecosystems providing services like water purification, flood control, biodiversity support, and carbon sequestration.
• The project aligns with India's commitments under the Ramsar Convention and other international environmental agreements.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
prelimsPoints: Definition and importance of wetlands.
Ramsar Convention and its objectives.
Examples of major Ramsar Sites in India.
Threats to wetlands.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological and economic significance of wetlands, highlighting the threats they face in India.
Analyze the provisions of the new national project for wetland conservation and its potential impact on biodiversity and local communities.
Examine the challenges in implementing integrated wetland management plans, including land-use changes, pollution, and lack of awareness.
Evaluate India's commitment to international conventions like the Ramsar Convention in its national conservation efforts.
articles.imageDescription: A serene wetland landscape with migratory birds and lush vegetation.
articles.trivia: Chilika Lake in Odisha is the largest brackish water lagoon in India and was the first Indian wetland of international importance designated under the Ramsar Convention in 1981.

articles.headline: NITI Aayog to Launch Comprehensive National Gender Equality Index
articles.explainer:
• NITI Aayog announced the development and upcoming launch of a National Gender Equality Index.
• The index aims to provide a robust framework for assessing progress on gender equality across states and union territories.
• It will measure various indicators related to health, education, economic participation, political representation, and protection from violence.
• The initiative seeks to identify disparities, track policy effectiveness, and foster competition among states for better gender outcomes.
• This data-driven approach is expected to inform policy interventions and resource allocation for women's empowerment.
gsSubTopics: Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies
Social empowerment, communalism, regionalism & secularism
prelimsPoints: Role of NITI Aayog in policy formulation and monitoring.
Key indicators of gender equality.
India's performance on global gender indices.
Government schemes for women empowerment (e.g., Beti Bachao Beti Padhao).
mainsPoints: Discuss the importance of a comprehensive Gender Equality Index in addressing gender disparities and promoting women's empowerment in India.
Analyze the multi-dimensional challenges that hinder gender equality in India, spanning social, economic, and political spheres.
Examine how a data-driven approach, such as a national index, can inform policy-making and resource allocation for better gender outcomes.
Evaluate the role of government initiatives, civil society, and community participation in achieving SDG 5 (Gender Equality) in India.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic depicting gender symbols and equality scales.
articles.trivia: The Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) include 'Gender Equality' as SDG 5, aiming to achieve gender equality and empower all women and girls by 2030.

articles.headline: Indian Navy Strengthens Maritime Domain Awareness (MDA) Capabilities in Indian Ocean Region
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Navy announced significant enhancements to its Maritime Domain Awareness (MDA) capabilities across the Indian Ocean Region (IOR).
• This includes the integration of advanced surveillance technologies, satellite imagery, and collaboration with littoral states.
• The move aims to bolster regional security, counter piracy, illegal fishing, and monitor suspicious activities in India's strategic maritime zones.
• Emphasis is placed on real-time information sharing and interoperability with friendly navies.
• Strengthening MDA is crucial for protecting India's economic interests, securing sea lines of communication, and projecting its naval power.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate
keyTerms: Information Fusion Centre – Indian Ocean Region (IFC-IOR)
prelimsPoints: Importance of IOR for India.
Role of Indian Navy in MDA.
Key maritime security challenges in IOR.
International collaborations for MDA (e.g., IFC-IOR).
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic significance of the Indian Ocean Region for India's security and economic prosperity.
Analyze the concept of Maritime Domain Awareness (MDA) and its critical role in safeguarding India's maritime interests.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for India in enhancing its MDA capabilities and fostering regional maritime security cooperation.
Evaluate the implications of strengthening MDA for India's naval power projection and its role as a net security provider in the IOR.
articles.imageDescription: An Indian Navy warship patrolling in the open sea.
articles.trivia: India's Information Fusion Centre – Indian Ocean Region (IFC-IOR) was established in 2018 at Gurugram to enhance maritime safety and security.

articles.headline: Government Launches National Mission on Climate-Resilient Agriculture to Boost Farmers' Adaptive Capacity
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare launched a new National Mission on Climate-Resilient Agriculture.
• The mission aims to equip farmers with strategies and technologies to adapt to the adverse impacts of climate change, such as erratic rainfall and extreme temperatures.
• Key components include promoting drought-resistant crop varieties, precision irrigation techniques, and climate-smart farming practices.
• It also focuses on integrating traditional knowledge with modern science to build local resilience and ensure food security.
• The initiative emphasizes research and development, capacity building, and farmer awareness programs.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints
Environmental pollution and degradation, conservation
Climate change
prelimsPoints: Objectives of the mission.
Key components of climate-resilient agriculture.
Impact of climate change on Indian agriculture.
Government initiatives for sustainable agriculture (e.g., Paramparagat Krishi Vikas Yojana).
mainsPoints: Discuss the vulnerability of Indian agriculture to climate change and its implications for food security and farmers' livelihoods.
Analyze the strategies and technologies being promoted under the National Mission on Climate-Resilient Agriculture.
Examine the challenges in adopting climate-resilient farming practices at the grassroots level and suggest policy interventions.
Evaluate the role of research, extension services, and farmer participation in building adaptive capacity in the agricultural sector.
articles.imageDescription: Farmers inspecting drought-resistant crops in a field.
articles.trivia: India is one of the world's largest agricultural producers, with agriculture contributing significantly to its GDP and employing a large portion of its population.

highlights: Punjab Floods: At least 30 reported killed, widespread damage; calls for enhanced disaster management and climate adaptation strategies.
Supreme Court Deliberates on Judicial Appointments: Hearings underway on transparency in Collegium system, potentially paving way for reforms.
RBI Maintains Repo Rate: Monetary Policy Committee keeps benchmark rate unchanged amidst inflation concerns and global economic uncertainties.
ISRO's Shukrayaan-1 Enters Venus Orbit: India's maiden Venus mission successfully begins scientific observations, marking a major space milestone.
National Green Hydrogen Mission Progress: Pilot projects show significant strides, government finalizing incentives to make India a global hub.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the National Green Hydrogen Mission, consider the following statements:
1. Its primary aim is to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
2. Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
3. The mission only focuses on the production of hydrogen and does not cover its utilization in various sectors.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: A primary aim of the National Green Hydrogen Mission is to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, contributing to energy security and decarbonization. Statement 2 is correct: Green hydrogen is indeed produced by the electrolysis of water using renewable energy sources. Statement 3 is incorrect: The mission covers both the production and utilization of green hydrogen across various sectors like steel, mobility, and fertilizers, as mentioned in the news and its broader objectives.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Collegium System in India:
1. It is a system under which judges are appointed by the President in consultation with the Chief Justice of India and other senior judges of the Supreme Court.
2. The National Judicial Appointments Commission (NJAC) Act, which sought to replace the Collegium System, was upheld by the Supreme Court.
3. The Supreme Court's deliberations on transparency in judicial appointments aim to maintain judicial independence while ensuring accountability.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The Collegium System involves the Chief Justice of India and a forum of four senior-most Supreme Court judges making recommendations for judicial appointments. Statement 2 is incorrect: The NJAC Act was struck down by the Supreme Court in 2015, declaring it unconstitutional. Statement 3 is correct: The current debate and Supreme Court deliberations are precisely about finding a balance between judicial independence, which the Collegium aims to protect, and greater transparency and accountability in appointments.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is responsible for determining the Minimum Support Price (MSP) for various crops in India?
options: (b) Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
(c) Commission for Agricultural Costs and Prices (CACP)
(d) National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development (NABARD)
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) Commission for Agricultural Costs and Prices (CACP)
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Commission for Agricultural Costs and Prices (CACP) is an attached office of the Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare. It advises the government on the price policy for major agricultural commodities, including recommending MSPs. The government then makes the final decision based on CACP recommendations.

prelimsMocks.question: With reference to India's space missions, Shukrayaan-1 is associated with which celestial body?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Shukrayaan-1 is India's maiden mission to Venus. The news states that the mission successfully entered Venusian orbit. ISRO has previously sent missions to the Moon (Chandrayaan series) and Mars (Mangalyaan/MOM).

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding Air Quality Index (AQI) in India:
1. AQI measures the concentration of only Particulate Matter (PM2.5 and PM10) in the air.
2. An AQI value of 0-50 is considered 'Good'.
3. The National Clean Air Programme (NCAP) aims to reduce particulate matter concentration by 20-30% by 2024.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect: AQI measures eight major pollutants: PM2.5, PM10, Nitrogen Dioxide (NO2), Sulfur Dioxide (SO2), Carbon Monoxide (CO), Ozone (O3), Ammonia (NH3), and Lead (Pb). Statement 2 is correct: An AQI value between 0-50 is indeed categorized as 'Good'. Statement 3 is correct: The NCAP launched in 2019 aims for a 20-30% reduction in PM2.5 and PM10 concentrations by 2024, with 2017 as the base year.

mainsMocks.question: Critically analyze the efficacy of India's current disaster management framework in addressing the challenges posed by increasing frequency and intensity of extreme weather events like floods. Suggest comprehensive measures to build climate-resilient infrastructure and enhance preparedness.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Disaster and disaster management; Environmental pollution and degradation, climate change.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce with the context of recent floods and their impact. Analyze the strengths (NDRF, early warning systems) and weaknesses (fragmented approach, lack of climate adaptation, infrastructure gaps) of the existing framework. Discuss specific measures for resilience, including floodplain zoning, green infrastructure, community participation, and inter-state cooperation. Conclude with a forward-looking statement on integrating climate action into disaster management.

mainsMocks.question: Discuss the ethical dilemmas and societal implications posed by the rapid advancement and deployment of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in India. How can a robust AI Ethics Framework balance innovation with the protection of individual rights and societal values?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life; Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by outlining the transformative potential of AI. Then, delve into ethical concerns like algorithmic bias, data privacy, job displacement, accountability, and the potential for misuse. Discuss the need for a framework that includes principles like fairness, transparency, explainability, human oversight, and privacy by design. Highlight the role of government, industry, and civil society in developing and enforcing such a framework, fostering responsible AI innovation.

mainsMocks.question: The debate around Centre-State fiscal relations often highlights concerns over fiscal autonomy and equitable resource distribution. In light of the recommendations of the Finance Commissions and the Goods and Services Tax (GST) regime, critically examine the challenges in India's fiscal federalism and suggest reforms to foster cooperative federalism.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the concept of fiscal federalism and the constitutional provisions. Discuss the role of Finance Commissions in horizontal and vertical devolution. Analyze challenges such as conditional grants, impact of GST on state revenues and autonomy (especially post-compensation period), and the increasing share of cesses and surcharges. Propose reforms for greater fiscal space for states, better consultation mechanisms, and a more robust dispute resolution framework to strengthen cooperative federalism.

quickBytes.text: The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) was established in 1960 to develop and maintain road networks in India's border areas.
The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) was established on April 1, 1935, under the RBI Act, 1934.
Article 280 of the Indian Constitution provides for the establishment of a Finance Commission every five years.
The National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 replaced the 1986 policy and aims for universalization of education from ECCE to secondary level.
The Ramsar Convention, signed in 1971, is an international treaty for the conservation and sustainable use of wetlands.
The Information Fusion Centre – Indian Ocean Region (IFC-IOR) is located in Gurugram, India, to enhance maritime safety and security.
The term 'Artificial Intelligence' was coined by John McCarthy in 1956.
The Commission for Agricultural Costs and Prices (CACP) recommends the Minimum Support Prices (MSPs) for various crops.
SDG 5 of the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals aims to achieve gender equality and empower all women and girls.
The 'Basic Structure Doctrine' of the Indian Constitution was propounded in the Kesavananda Bharati case (1973).

GS1.headline: NITI Aayog to Launch Comprehensive National Gender Equality Index
GS1.explainer:
• NITI Aayog announced the development and upcoming launch of a National Gender Equality Index.
• The index aims to provide a robust framework for assessing progress on gender equality across states and union territories.
• It will measure various indicators related to health, education, economic participation, political representation, and protection from violence.
• The initiative seeks to identify disparities, track policy effectiveness, and foster competition among states for better gender outcomes.
• This data-driven approach is expected to inform policy interventions and resource allocation for women's empowerment.
gsSubTopics: Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies
Social empowerment, communalism, regionalism & secularism
prelimsPoints: Role of NITI Aayog in policy formulation and monitoring.
Key indicators of gender equality.
India's performance on global gender indices.
Government schemes for women empowerment (e.g., Beti Bachao Beti Padhao).
mainsPoints: Discuss the importance of a comprehensive Gender Equality Index in addressing gender disparities and promoting women's empowerment in India.
Analyze the multi-dimensional challenges that hinder gender equality in India, spanning social, economic, and political spheres.
Examine how a data-driven approach, such as a national index, can inform policy-making and resource allocation for better gender outcomes.
Evaluate the role of government initiatives, civil society, and community participation in achieving SDG 5 (Gender Equality) in India.
GS1.imageDescription: A graphic depicting gender symbols and equality scales.
GS1.trivia: The Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) include 'Gender Equality' as SDG 5, aiming to achieve gender equality and empower all women and girls by 2030.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Deliberates on Transparency in Judicial Appointments: A Step Towards Collegium Reforms?
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court heard arguments on petitions seeking greater transparency and a more merit-based system for judicial appointments.
• Discussions centered on the need for a balanced approach that maintains judicial independence while ensuring accountability and public trust.
• Petitioners argued for clearer criteria, public disclosure of selection reasons, and a more diverse representation in the judiciary.
• The government's stance emphasized the need for a robust mechanism, potentially revisiting aspects of the National Judicial Appointments Commission (NJAC) debate.
• A verdict is anticipated which could significantly influence the future of the Collegium system.
gsSubTopics: Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary
Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies
prelimsPoints: Know about the Collegium system and its evolution.
Understand the NJAC and its fate.
Constitutional provisions related to appointment of judges.
Difference between appointment of SC and HC judges.
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate the Collegium system for judicial appointments in India, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses.
Discuss the various proposals for reforming judicial appointments, considering both judicial independence and accountability.
Examine the role of the judiciary in upholding constitutional principles amidst debates on its own structure and functioning.
Analyze the implications of greater transparency in judicial appointments for public trust and the quality of justice.
GS2.imageDescription: Supreme Court of India building in New Delhi.
GS2.trivia: The 'Basic Structure Doctrine' of the Indian Constitution was propounded by the Supreme Court in the Kesavananda Bharati case in 1973.

GS2.headline: India-Africa Economic Forum Concludes, Pledges Enhanced Cooperation in Trade and Technology
GS2.explainer:
• The annual India-Africa Economic Forum concluded with a joint declaration emphasizing deeper collaboration in critical sectors.
• Key areas of focus included digital transformation, renewable energy, healthcare, and agricultural development.
• India reaffirmed its commitment to capacity building and technology transfer to African nations.
• Discussions also covered improving trade logistics, reducing tariff barriers, and promoting private sector investments.
• The forum aims to strengthen India's strategic partnerships across the African continent.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: Key pillars of India-Africa cooperation.
Major Indian initiatives in Africa (e.g., Pan-Africa e-network project).
Importance of Africa for India's energy security and market access.
Recent India-Africa summits and declarations.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolving contours of India-Africa relations, highlighting the shift from traditional aid to partnership-based cooperation.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities for India in expanding its economic and strategic footprint in Africa.
Examine how India's 'development partnership' model differs from that of other major global powers in Africa.
Evaluate the potential of digital and green transitions to drive India-Africa collaboration.
GS2.imageDescription: Delegates from India and African nations at a conference.
GS2.trivia: India is one of the largest contributors of troops to UN peacekeeping missions in Africa.

GS2.headline: Review Meeting Assesses National Education Policy Implementation Progress and Challenges
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education held a high-level review meeting to assess the implementation status of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
• Discussions focused on the transition to multidisciplinary education, vocational training integration, and early childhood care and education (ECCE).
• Challenges identified include infrastructure gaps, faculty training, and ensuring equitable access to quality education, especially in rural areas.
• States presented their progress reports, highlighting innovative approaches and areas requiring further central support.
• The government reiterated its commitment to achieving the NEP's transformative goals by 2030.
gsSubTopics: Development processes and the development industry—the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
prelimsPoints: Key pillars and objectives of NEP 2020.
Recommendations for school and higher education.
Focus on ECCE and vocational education.
Role of technology in NEP implementation.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the transformative potential of NEP 2020 in addressing the challenges of India's education system.
Discuss the key challenges in the effective implementation of NEP 2020, particularly concerning infrastructure, faculty readiness, and equitable access.
Examine how the integration of vocational education and multidisciplinary learning can enhance employability and human resource development.
Evaluate the role of state governments and private institutions in achieving the goals of the National Education Policy.
GS2.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom setting.
GS2.trivia: The first National Policy on Education in India was promulgated in 1968, based on the recommendations of the Kothari Commission (1964-66).

GS2.headline: India Reiterates Call for Comprehensive UN Reforms Ahead of General Assembly Session
GS2.explainer:
• India's External Affairs Minister reiterated the country's long-standing demand for comprehensive reforms of the United Nations, particularly the Security Council.
• The call comes ahead of the upcoming UN General Assembly session, where the issue of multilateralism and global governance is expected to be a key theme.
• India emphasized the need for a more representative and equitable Security Council that reflects contemporary geopolitical realities.
• The statement highlighted the growing consensus among developing nations for reforms that enhance the legitimacy and effectiveness of the UN.
• India's bid for a permanent seat in the UNSC remains a central aspect of its foreign policy objectives.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
prelimsPoints: Structure and functions of the UN Security Council.
Current permanent and non-permanent members of UNSC.
Arguments for and against UNSC expansion.
Role of G4 nations in UN reforms.
mainsPoints: Discuss the rationale behind India's persistent demand for reforms in the United Nations Security Council.
Analyze the challenges and prospects for comprehensive UN reforms in the current geopolitical landscape.
Examine how a reformed UNSC could better address contemporary global challenges such as climate change, pandemics, and terrorism.
Evaluate the implications of India gaining a permanent seat in the UNSC for its foreign policy and global influence.
GS2.imageDescription: United Nations Headquarters building in New York.
GS2.trivia: The United Nations was founded in 1945 after World War II to prevent future wars, replacing the ineffective League of Nations.

GS2.headline: Centre-State Fiscal Relations Under Scrutiny: Debate on Autonomy and Equity in Fiscal Transfers
GS2.explainer:
• A renewed debate has emerged regarding the existing framework of fiscal transfers between the Centre and states, particularly concerning grants and centrally sponsored schemes.
• Several states have voiced concerns over reduced fiscal autonomy and conditionalities attached to central funds.
• The discussion highlights the recommendations of previous Finance Commissions and the need for a balanced approach to federal fiscal relations.
• Experts are deliberating on mechanisms to ensure greater equity in resource distribution while incentivizing fiscal discipline among states.
• The role of GST compensation and its future implications for state finances is also a key point of contention.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein
prelimsPoints: Constitutional provisions for Centre-State financial relations.
Role and functions of the Finance Commission.
Difference between grants-in-aid and shared taxes.
Impact of GST on state finances.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the evolution of fiscal federalism in India, highlighting the challenges in ensuring both fiscal autonomy for states and national uniformity.
Discuss the recommendations of recent Finance Commissions regarding fiscal transfers and their effectiveness in addressing inter-state disparities.
Examine the implications of the Goods and Services Tax (GST) regime on the fiscal powers and responsibilities of state governments.
Suggest reforms to the Centre-state fiscal relations framework to foster cooperative federalism and equitable development.
GS2.imageDescription: Indian Parliament building, symbolizing federal governance.
GS2.trivia: Article 280 of the Indian Constitution provides for the establishment of a Finance Commission.

GS2.headline: Government Launches New Scheme to Enhance Healthcare Access in Remote Rural Areas
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare introduced a new comprehensive scheme aimed at bridging the healthcare gap in remote and underserved rural areas.
• The scheme focuses on strengthening primary healthcare centers, deploying mobile medical units, and leveraging telemedicine technologies.
• Special incentives are being offered to medical professionals to serve in rural postings, addressing the critical shortage of healthcare staff.
• Community health workers will receive enhanced training and resources to deliver preventive care and health education.
• The initiative is part of India's commitment to achieving universal health coverage and improving health outcomes for all citizens.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
prelimsPoints: Key components of the new scheme.
Challenges in rural healthcare delivery in India.
Government flagship health schemes (e.g., Ayushman Bharat, National Health Mission).
Role of technology in improving healthcare access.
mainsPoints: Discuss the persistent challenges in providing equitable and quality healthcare access to India's rural population.
Analyze the potential of telemedicine and mobile medical units in transforming rural healthcare delivery.
Examine the effectiveness of government schemes and incentives in attracting and retaining medical professionals in rural areas.
Suggest comprehensive strategies to achieve universal health coverage in India, considering both infrastructure and human resource development.
GS2.imageDescription: A mobile medical unit serving patients in a remote Indian village.
GS2.trivia: India is home to one of the world's largest public health programs, the National Health Mission (NHM), which encompasses the National Rural Health Mission (NRHM) and National Urban Health Mission (NUHM).

GS3.headline: Punjab Grapples with Devastating Floods: Calls for Enhanced Disaster Management and Climate Adaptation
GS3.explainer:
• Heavy rainfall over several days has led to widespread flooding across Punjab, affecting multiple districts and causing significant loss of life and property.
• At least 30 fatalities have been reported, with thousands displaced and extensive damage to agricultural land and infrastructure.
• The state government has launched extensive relief and rescue operations, deploying NDRF and SDRF teams.
• Experts highlight the role of climate change, inadequate drainage systems, and encroachments on floodplains in exacerbating the disaster.
• Discussions are underway regarding long-term strategies for flood control, early warning systems, and climate-resilient infrastructure.
gsSubTopics: Disaster and disaster management
Environmental pollution and degradation
Conservation
Climate change
prelimsPoints: Location of affected areas in Punjab.
Role of NDRF and SDRF in disaster response.
Causes of flooding: heavy rainfall, drainage issues, climate change.
Government initiatives for flood control and relief.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze India's disaster management framework in the context of recent floods, suggesting improvements.
Discuss the interlinkages between climate change, urban planning, and increasing frequency of extreme weather events in India.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current infrastructure in mitigating flood risks and propose strategies for building climate-resilient infrastructure.
Examine the challenges in inter-state cooperation for river basin management and flood control.
GS3.imageDescription: Aerial view of flooded agricultural fields and submerged villages in Punjab.
GS3.trivia: The Bhakra Dam, one of the largest multi-purpose dams in India, is located on the Sutlej River in Punjab and Himachal Pradesh, playing a crucial role in flood moderation and irrigation.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Inflationary Concerns and Global Slowdown
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) concluded its latest Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) meeting, opting to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged.
• The decision comes against a backdrop of persistent core inflation, though food inflation shows signs of moderation.
• RBI Governor highlighted concerns over global economic uncertainties and their potential impact on India's growth trajectory.
• The central bank reaffirmed its commitment to withdrawing accommodation gradually to ensure inflation remains within the target band.
• Analysts predict a cautious stance will continue as the RBI navigates between supporting growth and taming inflation.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary policy
prelimsPoints: Functions of RBI and MPC.
Definition of Repo Rate, Reverse Repo Rate, CRR, SLR.
Tools of monetary policy.
Inflation targets set by RBI.
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control and economic growth in the current global economic scenario.
Discuss the effectiveness of monetary policy tools in addressing supply-side inflation in India.
Examine the coordination between fiscal and monetary policy in achieving macroeconomic stability.
Evaluate the impact of global economic slowdowns on India's monetary policy decisions.
GS3.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India building in Mumbai.
GS3.trivia: The Reserve Bank of India was established on April 1, 1935, in accordance with the provisions of the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

GS3.headline: ISRO's Shukrayaan-1 Mission Successfully Enters Venusian Orbit, Begins Scientific Observations
GS3.explainer:
• India's maiden mission to Venus, Shukrayaan-1, has successfully performed the crucial Venus Orbit Insertion (VOI) maneuver.
• The spacecraft is now orbiting Venus and has commenced its primary scientific objectives, including studying the planet's atmosphere and surface.
• The mission aims to investigate Venus's atmospheric composition, surface features, and subsurface structure using advanced instruments.
• This achievement marks a significant milestone for ISRO, expanding its planetary exploration capabilities.
• Data from Shukrayaan-1 is expected to provide valuable insights into planetary evolution and habitability.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Space
prelimsPoints: Objectives of Shukrayaan-1 mission.
Key instruments on board Shukrayaan-1.
About Venus: 'Earth's twin', atmospheric composition.
Other countries' missions to Venus.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of India's Venus mission for its space program and global scientific community.
Examine how space exploration contributes to scientific knowledge and technological advancement, with specific reference to ISRO's achievements.
Analyze the challenges involved in interplanetary missions and how ISRO has overcome them.
Evaluate the geopolitical implications of India's growing capabilities in space technology.
GS3.imageDescription: Artistic rendering of ISRO's Shukrayaan-1 spacecraft orbiting Venus.
GS3.trivia: Venus rotates in the opposite direction to most other planets in our solar system, a phenomenon known as retrograde rotation.

GS3.headline: National Green Hydrogen Mission Reports Significant Progress in Pilot Projects and Policy Framework
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy announced substantial progress in the implementation of the National Green Hydrogen Mission.
• Several pilot projects for green hydrogen production and utilization, including in steel and mobility sectors, have commenced operations.
• The government is finalizing incentives and regulatory frameworks to attract private investment and boost domestic manufacturing of electrolysers.
• The mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, contributing to energy security and decarbonization.
• Challenges related to cost-effectiveness, infrastructure development, and water availability for electrolysis remain key areas of focus.
gsSubTopics: Environmental pollution and degradation
Conservation
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: Objectives of the National Green Hydrogen Mission.
Difference between green, blue, and grey hydrogen.
Applications of green hydrogen (e.g., fertilizers, steel, transport).
Key technologies involved in green hydrogen production.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and climate change commitments.
Analyze the challenges in scaling up green hydrogen production and adoption in India, suggesting policy interventions.
Evaluate the potential of green hydrogen to transform key industrial sectors and its role in achieving net-zero targets.
Examine the geopolitical implications of India becoming a major player in the global green hydrogen market.
GS3.imageDescription: An illustration depicting green hydrogen production facility with wind turbines and solar panels.
GS3.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, constituting roughly 75% of all baryonic mass.

GS3.headline: Parliamentary Standing Committee Recommends Comprehensive Review of MSP System for Crop Diversification
GS3.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee submitted its report on agricultural reforms, advocating for a comprehensive review of the Minimum Support Price (MSP) mechanism.
• The report suggests linking MSP to crop diversification efforts and promoting cultivation of less water-intensive crops.
• It highlighted the need to address regional imbalances in MSP procurement and ensure benefits reach all farmers.
• Recommendations include strengthening agricultural market infrastructure and exploring alternative income support schemes.
• The committee emphasized a consultative approach involving farmers' organizations in policy formulation.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints
E-technology in the aid of farmers
Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices
keyTerms: Commission for Agricultural Costs and Prices (CACP)
prelimsPoints: How MSP is determined and by whom (CACP).
Number of crops under MSP.
Objectives of MSP.
Challenges associated with the current MSP system.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the role of MSP in ensuring farmers' income and food security in India, highlighting its unintended consequences.
Discuss the feasibility and potential benefits of linking MSP to crop diversification and sustainable agricultural practices.
Examine the reforms needed in India's agricultural marketing system to ensure better price realization for farmers and reduce post-harvest losses.
Evaluate alternative income support mechanisms for farmers in the context of MSP reforms.
GS3.imageDescription: Farmers working in a field with various crops.
GS3.trivia: The concept of MSP was introduced in India in the mid-1960s to incentivize farmers for adopting high-yielding varieties of crops during the Green Revolution.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils New National Cyber Security Framework to Bolster Digital Defenses
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) launched a comprehensive National Cyber Security Framework.
• The framework aims to create a secure and resilient cyber ecosystem by establishing new guidelines for critical infrastructure protection, data governance, and incident response.
• It emphasizes public-private partnerships, skill development, and international cooperation to combat evolving cyber threats.
• The policy introduces stricter compliance requirements for organizations handling sensitive data and mandates regular security audits.
• This move is crucial for safeguarding India's digital economy and national security interests.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security
Money-laundering and its prevention
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism
prelimsPoints: Key objectives of the new framework.
Role of CERT-In (Indian Computer Emergency Response Team).
Concept of Critical Information Infrastructure (CII).
Major cyber threats faced by India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the increasing complexities of cyber threats and the need for a robust national cyber security framework in India.
Analyze the key provisions of the new framework and its potential impact on data governance and critical infrastructure protection.
Examine the role of public-private partnerships in building a resilient cyber ecosystem in India.
Evaluate the challenges in implementing a comprehensive cyber security strategy, including skill gaps and cross-border cooperation.
GS3.imageDescription: A digital representation of a secure network with interconnected nodes.
GS3.trivia: The first computer virus, 'Brain', was created in 1986 by two Pakistani brothers and targeted IBM PC compatible computers.

GS3.headline: Government Announces Accelerated Infrastructure Spending to Boost Economic Growth and Job Creation
GS3.explainer:
• The Finance Ministry unveiled plans to significantly accelerate infrastructure spending in key sectors like roads, railways, and urban development.
• The move aims to stimulate economic activity, create employment opportunities, and enhance India's global competitiveness.
• Emphasis will be on projects with high multiplier effects and those that integrate green technologies and sustainable practices.
• Public-private partnerships (PPPs) are expected to play a crucial role, with new policy incentives for private sector participation.
• This initiative aligns with India's long-term vision of becoming a $5 trillion economy.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Investment models
prelimsPoints: Key sectors targeted for infrastructure spending.
Concept of PPPs and their importance.
Government initiatives like National Infrastructure Pipeline (NIP) and PM Gati Shakti.
Impact of infrastructure on GDP and employment.
mainsPoints: Discuss the critical role of infrastructure development in achieving India's economic growth targets and improving quality of life.
Analyze the challenges in financing and executing large-scale infrastructure projects in India, and suggest solutions.
Evaluate the effectiveness of public-private partnerships (PPPs) as a model for infrastructure development in the Indian context.
Examine how sustainable and green infrastructure can contribute to both economic development and environmental protection.
GS3.imageDescription: Construction site of a modern highway with heavy machinery.
GS3.trivia: India has the second-largest road network in the world, spanning over 6.3 million kilometers.

GS3.headline: Urban Local Bodies Launch New Initiatives to Combat Air Pollution in Major Cities
GS3.explainer:
• Several urban local bodies across India have rolled out enhanced action plans to improve air quality in their respective cities.
• Initiatives include deploying advanced air quality monitoring systems, promoting electric vehicles, and stricter enforcement of industrial emission norms.
• Focus is also on managing construction and demolition waste, controlling vehicular pollution, and increasing green cover.
• Public awareness campaigns are being intensified to encourage citizen participation in pollution reduction efforts.
• These measures are part of the broader National Clean Air Programme (NCAP) goals.
gsSubTopics: Environmental pollution and degradation
Conservation
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: Objectives of NCAP.
Components of Air Quality Index (AQI) and its categories.
Major sources of urban air pollution.
Government policies to control air pollution (e.g., BS norms, FAME India scheme).
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted challenges of urban air pollution in India and its impact on public health and the environment.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current government initiatives, such as NCAP, in achieving cleaner air targets.
Examine the role of urban local bodies and citizen participation in developing and implementing effective air pollution control strategies.
Suggest innovative technological and policy solutions to combat air pollution in Indian cities.
GS3.imageDescription: A cityscape shrouded in smog with air quality monitoring equipment in the foreground.
GS3.trivia: Delhi has consistently ranked among the most polluted cities globally, prompting various emergency measures like the 'odd-even' road rationing scheme.

GS3.headline: NITI Aayog Convenes Stakeholders for Deliberations on India's AI Ethics Framework
GS3.explainer:
• NITI Aayog hosted a crucial meeting with experts, industry leaders, and civil society organizations to discuss the draft AI Ethics Framework.
• The framework aims to ensure responsible and ethical development and deployment of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in India.
